19: 20: Mastodon is a **free, open-source social network server** based on [ActivityPub](https://www.w3.org/TR/activitypub/) where users can follow friends and discover new ones. On Mastodon, users can publish anything they want: links, pictures, text, and video. All Mastodon servers are interoperable as a federated network (users on one server can seamlessly communicate with users from another one, including non-Mastodon software that implements ActivityPub!) 21: 22: ## Navigation 23: 24: - [Project homepage 🐘](https://joinmastodon.org) 25: - [Donate to support development 🎁](https://joinmastodon.org/sponsors#donate) 26: - [View sponsors](https://joinmastodon.org/sponsors) 27: - [Blog 📰](https://blog.joinmastodon.org) 28: - [Documentation 📚](https://docs.joinmastodon.org) 29: - [Official container image 🚢](https://github.com/mastodon/mastodon/pkgs/container/mastodon) 30: 31: ## Features 32: 33: 34: 35: **Part of the Fediverse. Based on open standards, with no vendor lock-in.** - the network goes beyond just Mastodon; anything that implements ActivityPub is part of a broader social network known as [the Fediverse](https://jointhefediverse.net/). You can follow and interact with users on other servers (including those running different software), and they can follow you back. 36: 37: **Real-time, chronological timeline updates** - updates of people you're following appear in real-time in the UI. 38: 39: **Media attachments** - upload and view images and videos attached to the updates. Videos with no audio track are treated like animated GIFs; normal videos loop continuously. 40: 41: **Safety and moderation tools** - Mastodon includes private posts, locked accounts, phrase filtering, muting, blocking, and many other features, along with a reporting and moderation system. 42: 43: **OAuth2 and a straightforward REST API** - Mastodon acts as an OAuth2 provider, and third party apps can use the REST and Streaming APIs. This results in a [rich app ecosystem](https://joinmastodon.org/apps) with a variety of choices! 44: 45: ## Deployment 46: 47: ### Tech stack 48: 49: - [Ruby on Rails](https://github.com/rails/rails) powers the REST API and other web pages. 50: - [PostgreSQL](https://www.postgresql.org/) is the main database. 51: - [Redis](https://redis.io/) and [Sidekiq](https://sidekiq.org/) are used for caching and queueing. 52: - [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) powers the streaming API. 53: - [React.js](https://reactjs.org/) and [Redux](https://redux.js.org/) are used for the dynamic parts of the interface. 54: - [BrowserStack](https://www.browserstack.com/) supports testing on real devices and browsers. 1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Status::SafeReblogInsert
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   class_methods do
7:     # This patch overwrites the built-in ActiveRecord `_insert_record` method to
8:     # ensure that no reblogs of discarded statuses are created, as this cannot be
9:     # enforced through DB constraints the same way as reblogs of deleted statuses
10:     #
11:     # We redefine the internal method responsible for issuing the `INSERT`
12:     # statement and replace the `INSERT INTO ... VALUES ...` query with an `INSERT
13:     # INTO ... SELECT ...` query with a `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL` clause on the
14:     # reblogged status to ensure consistency at the database level.
15:     #
16:     # The code is kept similar to ActiveRecord::Persistence code and calls it
17:     # directly when we are not handling a reblog.
18:     #
19:     # https://github.com/rails/rails/blob/v8.0.2/activerecord/lib/active_record/persistence.rb#L238-L261
20:     def _insert_record(connection, values, returning)
21:       return super unless values.is_a?(Hash) && values['reblog_of_id']&.value.present?
22: 
23:       primary_key = self.primary_key
24:       primary_key_value = nil
25: 
26:       if prefetch_primary_key? && primary_key
27:         values[primary_key] ||= begin
28:           primary_key_value = next_sequence_value
29:           _default_attributes[primary_key].with_cast_value(primary_key_value)
30:         end
31:       end
32: 
33:       # The following line departs from stock ActiveRecord
34:       # Original code was:
35:       # im = Arel::InsertManager.new(arel_table)
36:       # Instead, we use a custom builder when a reblog is happening:
37:       im = _compile_reblog_insert(values)
38: 
39:       connection.insert(
40:         im, "#{self} Create", primary_key || false, primary_key_value,
41:         returning: returning
42:       ).tap do |result|
43:         # Since we are using SELECT instead of VALUES, a non-error `nil` return is possible.
44:         # For our purposes, it's equivalent to a foreign key constraint violation
45:         raise ActiveRecord::InvalidForeignKey, "(reblog_of_id)=(#{values['reblog_of_id'].value}) is not present in table \"statuses\"" if result.nil?
46:       end
47:     end
48: 
49:     def _compile_reblog_insert(values)
50:       # This is somewhat equivalent to the following code of ActiveRecord::Persistence:
51:       # `arel_table.compile_insert(_substitute_values(values))`
52:       # The main difference is that we use a `SELECT` instead of a `VALUES` clause,
53:       # which means we have to build the `SELECT` clause ourselves and do a bit more
54:       # manual work.
55: 
56:       # Instead of using Arel::InsertManager#values, we are going to use Arel::InsertManager#select
57:       im = Arel::InsertManager.new
58:       im.into(arel_table)
59: 
60:       binds = []
61:       reblog_bind = nil
62:       values.each do |name, attribute|
63:         attr = arel_table[name]
64:         bind = predicate_builder.build_bind_attribute(attr.name, attribute.value)
65: 
66:         im.columns << attr
67:         binds << bind
68: 
69:         reblog_bind = bind if name == 'reblog_of_id'
70:       end
71: 
72:       im.select(arel_table.where(arel_table[:id].eq(reblog_bind)).where(arel_table[:deleted_at].eq(nil)).project(*binds))
73: 
74:       im
75:     end
76:   end
77: end

3: module Status::ThreadingConcern
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   class_methods do
7:     def permitted_statuses_from_ids(ids, account, stable: false)
8:       statuses    = Status.with_accounts(ids).to_a
9:       account_ids = statuses.map(&:account_id).uniq
10:       domains     = statuses.filter_map(&:account_domain).uniq
11: 
12:       account&.preload_relations!(account_ids, domains)
13: 
14:       statuses.reject! { |status| StatusFilter.new(status, account).filtered? }
15: 
16:       if stable
17:         statuses.sort_by! { |status| ids.index(status.id) }
18:       else
19:         statuses
20:       end
21:     end
22:   end
23: 
24:   def ancestors(limit, account = nil)
25:     find_statuses_from_tree_path(ancestor_ids(limit), account)
26:   end
27:

30:   end

44:       Rails.cache.write key, limit: limit, ids: ids
45:       ids
46:     else

48:     end

52:     Status.find_by_sql([<<~SQL.squish, id: in_reply_to_id, limit: limit])
53:       WITH RECURSIVE search_tree(id, in_reply_to_id, path)
54:       AS (
55:         SELECT id, in_reply_to_id, ARRAY[id]
56:         FROM statuses
57:         WHERE id = :id
58:         UNION ALL
59:         SELECT statuses.id, statuses.in_reply_to_id, path || statuses.id
60:         FROM search_tree
61:         JOIN statuses ON statuses.id = search_tree.in_reply_to_id
62:         WHERE NOT statuses.id = ANY(path)
63:       )
64:       SELECT id
65:       FROM search_tree
66:       ORDER BY path
67:       LIMIT :limit
68:     SQL

73:     depth += 1 if depth.present?
74:     limit += 1 if limit.present?

76:     descendants_with_self = Status.find_by_sql([<<~SQL.squish, id: id, limit: limit, depth: depth])
77:       WITH RECURSIVE search_tree(id, path) AS (
78:         SELECT id, ARRAY[id]
79:         FROM statuses
80:         WHERE id = :id
81:       UNION ALL
82:         SELECT statuses.id, path || statuses.id
83:         FROM search_tree
84:         JOIN statuses ON statuses.in_reply_to_id = search_tree.id
85:         WHERE COALESCE(array_length(path, 1) < :depth, TRUE) AND NOT statuses.id = ANY(path)
86:       )
87:       SELECT id
88:       FROM search_tree
89:       ORDER BY path
90:       LIMIT :limit

100:     if promote
101:       promote_by!(statuses) { |status| status.in_reply_to_account_id == status.account_id }
102:     else
103:       statuses
104:     end

107:   def promote_by!(arr)
108:     insert_at = arr.find_index { |item| !yield(item) }
109: 
110:     return arr if insert_at.nil?
111: 
112:     arr.each_with_index do |item, index|
113:       next if index <= insert_at || !yield(item)
114: 
115:       arr.insert(insert_at, arr.delete_at(index))
116:       insert_at += 1
117:     end
118: 
119:     arr
120:   end

4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   included do
7:     composed_of :quote_interaction_policy, class_name: 'InteractionPolicy', mapping: { quote_approval_policy: :bitmap }
8:

10:   end
11: 
12:   def quote_policy_as_keys(kind)
13:     raise ArgumentError unless kind.in?(%i(automatic manual))
14: 
15:     sub_policy = quote_interaction_policy.send(kind)
16:     sub_policy.as_keys
17:   end
18: 
19:   # Returns `:automatic`, `:manual`, `:unknown` or `:denied`
20:   def quote_policy_for_account(other_account)
21:     return :denied if other_account.nil? || direct_visibility? || reblog?
22: 
23:     following_author = nil
24:     followed_by_author = nil
25: 
26:     # Post author is always allowed to quote themselves
27:     return :automatic if account_id == other_account.id
28: 
29:     automatic_policy = quote_interaction_policy.automatic
30: 
31:     return :automatic if automatic_policy.public?
32: 
33:     if automatic_policy.followers?
34:       following_author = other_account.following?(account) if following_author.nil?
35:       return :automatic if following_author
36:     end
37: 
38:     if automatic_policy.following?
39:       followed_by_author = account.following?(other_account) if followed_by_author.nil?
40:       return :automatic if followed_by_author
41:     end
42: 
43:     # We don't know we are allowed by the automatic policy, considering the manual one
44:     manual_policy = quote_interaction_policy.manual
45: 
46:     return :manual if manual_policy.public?
47: 
48:     if manual_policy.followers?
49:       following_author = other_account.following?(account) if following_author.nil?
50:       return :manual if following_author
51:     end
52: 
53:     if manual_policy.following?
54:       followed_by_author = account.following?(other_account) if followed_by_author.nil?
55:       return :manual if followed_by_author
56:     end
57: 
58:     return :unknown if [automatic_policy, manual_policy].any?(&:unsupported_policy?)
59: 
60:     :denied
61:   end
62:

64:     self.quote_approval_policy = 0
65:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: module Paginable
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   included do
7:     scope :paginate_by_max_id, lambda { |limit, max_id = nil, since_id = nil|
8:       query = order(arel_table[:id].desc).limit(limit)
9:       query = query.where(arel_table[:id].lt(max_id)) if max_id.present?
10:       query = query.where(arel_table[:id].gt(since_id)) if since_id.present?
11:       query
12:     }
13: 
14:     # Differs from :paginate_by_max_id in that it gives the results immediately following min_id,
15:     # whereas since_id gives the items with largest id, but with since_id as a cutoff.
16:     # Results will be in ascending order by id.
17:     scope :paginate_by_min_id, lambda { |limit, min_id = nil, max_id = nil|
18:       query = reorder(arel_table[:id]).limit(limit)
19:       query = query.where(arel_table[:id].gt(min_id)) if min_id.present?
20:       query = query.where(arel_table[:id].lt(max_id)) if max_id.present?
21:       query
22:     }
23: 
24:     def self.to_a_paginated_by_id(limit, options = {})
25:       if options[:min_id].present?
26:         paginate_by_min_id(limit, options[:min_id], options[:max_id]).reverse
27:       else
28:         paginate_by_max_id(limit, options[:max_id], options[:since_id]).to_a
29:       end
30:     end
31:   end
32: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Status::FetchRepliesConcern
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   # debounce fetching all replies to minimize DoS
7:   # Period to wait between fetching replies
8:   FETCH_REPLIES_COOLDOWN_MINUTES = 15.minutes
9:   # Period to wait after a post is first created before fetching its replies
10:   FETCH_REPLIES_INITIAL_WAIT_MINUTES = 5.minutes
11: 
12:   included do
13:     scope :created_recently, -> { where(created_at: FETCH_REPLIES_INITIAL_WAIT_MINUTES.ago..) }
14:     scope :not_created_recently, -> { where(created_at: ..FETCH_REPLIES_INITIAL_WAIT_MINUTES.ago) }
15:     scope :fetched_recently, -> { where(fetched_replies_at: FETCH_REPLIES_COOLDOWN_MINUTES.ago..) }
16:     scope :not_fetched_recently, -> { where(fetched_replies_at: [nil, ..FETCH_REPLIES_COOLDOWN_MINUTES.ago]) }
17: 
18:     scope :should_not_fetch_replies, -> { local.or(created_recently.or(fetched_recently)) }
19:     scope :should_fetch_replies, -> { remote.not_created_recently.not_fetched_recently }
20: 
21:     # statuses for which we won't receive update or deletion actions,
22:     # and should update when fetching replies
23:     # Status from an account which either
24:     # a) has only remote followers
25:     # b) has local follows that were created after the last update time, or
26:     # c) has no known followers
27:     scope :unsubscribed, lambda {
28:       remote.merge(
29:         Status.left_outer_joins(account: :followers).where.not(followers_accounts: { domain: nil })
30:               .or(where.not('follows.created_at < statuses.updated_at'))
31:               .or(where(follows: { id: nil }))
32:       )
33:     }
34:   end
35: 
36:   def should_fetch_replies?
37:     # we aren't brand new, and we haven't fetched replies since the debounce window
38:     !local? && distributable? && created_at <= FETCH_REPLIES_INITIAL_WAIT_MINUTES.ago && (
39:       fetched_replies_at.nil? || fetched_replies_at <= FETCH_REPLIES_COOLDOWN_MINUTES.ago
40:     )
41:   end
42: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class PostStatusService < BaseService
4:   include Redisable
5:   include Lockable
6:   include LanguagesHelper
7: 
8:   # How much to delay sending an e-mail about a new post, to allow grouping multiple posts
9:   EMAIL_DISTRIBUTION_DELAY = 5.minutes.freeze
10: 
11:   # If the job is not executed within this timeframe, it will lose its arguments
12:   EMAIL_DISTRIBUTION_TTL = 1.hour.to_i
13: 
14:   class UnexpectedMentionsError < StandardError
15:     attr_reader :accounts
16: 
17:     def initialize(message, accounts)
18:       super(message)
19:       @accounts = accounts
20:     end
21:   end
22: 
23:   # Post a text status update, fetch and notify remote users mentioned
24:   # @param [Account] account Account from which to post
25:   # @param [Hash] options
26:   # @option [String] :text Message
27:   # @option [Status] :thread Optional status to reply to
28:   # @option [Status] :quoted_status Optional status to quote
29:   # @option [String] :quote_approval_policy Approval policy for quotes, one of `public`, `followers` or `nobody`
30:   # @option [Boolean] :sensitive
31:   # @option [String] :visibility
32:   # @option [String] :spoiler_text
33:   # @option [String] :language
34:   # @option [String] :scheduled_at
35:   # @option [Hash] :poll Optional poll to attach
36:   # @option [Enumerable] :media_ids Optional array of media IDs to attach
37:   # @option [Doorkeeper::Application] :application
38:   # @option [String] :idempotency Optional idempotency key
39:   # @option [Boolean] :with_rate_limit
40:   # @option [Enumerable] :allowed_mentions Optional array of expected mentioned account IDs, raises `UnexpectedMentionsError` if unexpected accounts end up in mentions
41:   # @return [Status]
42:   def call(account, options = {})
43:     @account     = account
44:     @options     = options
45:     @text        = @options[:text] || ''
46:     @in_reply_to = @options[:thread]
47:     @quoted_status = @options[:quoted_status]
48: 
49:     with_idempotency do
50:       validate_media!
51:       preprocess_attributes!
52: 
53:       if scheduled?
54:         schedule_status!
55:       else
56:         process_status!
57:       end
58:     end
59: 
60:     unless scheduled?
61:       postprocess_status!
62:       bump_potential_friendship!
63:     end
64: 
65:     @status
66:   rescue Antispam::SilentlyDrop => e
67:     e.status
68:   end
69: 
70:   private
71: 
72:   def preprocess_attributes!
73:     @sensitive    = (@options[:sensitive].nil? ? @account.user&.setting_default_sensitive : @options[:sensitive]) || @options[:spoiler_text].present?
74:     @text         = @options.delete(:spoiler_text) if @text.blank? && @options[:spoiler_text].present? && @quoted_status.blank?
75:     @visibility   = @options[:visibility] || @account.user&.setting_default_privacy
76:     @visibility   = :unlisted if @visibility&.to_sym == :public && @account.silenced?
77:     @visibility   = :private if @quoted_status&.private_visibility? && %i(public unlisted).include?(@visibility&.to_sym)
78:     @scheduled_at = @options[:scheduled_at]&.to_datetime
79:     @scheduled_at = nil if scheduled_in_the_past?
80:   rescue ArgumentError
81:     raise ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid
82:   end
83: 
84:   def process_status!
85:     @status = @account.statuses.new(status_attributes)
86:     process_mentions_service.call(@status)
87:     safeguard_mentions!(@status)
88:     safeguard_private_mention_quote!(@status)
89:     attach_tagged_objects!(@status)
90:     attach_quote!(@status)
91: 
92:     antispam = Antispam.new(@status)
93:     antispam.local_preflight_check!
94: 
95:     # The following transaction block is needed to wrap the UPDATEs to
96:     # the media attachments when the status is created
97:     ApplicationRecord.transaction do
98:       @status.save!
99:     end
100:   end
101: 
102:   def safeguard_private_mention_quote!(status)
103:     return if @quoted_status.nil? || @visibility.to_sym != :direct
104: 
105:     # The mentions array test here is awkward because the relationship is not persisted at this time
106:     return if @quoted_status.account_id == @account.id || status.mentions.to_a.any? { |mention| mention.account_id == @quoted_status.account_id && !mention.silent }
107: 
108:     status.errors.add(:base, I18n.t('statuses.errors.quoted_user_not_mentioned'))
109:     raise ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid, status
110:   end
111: 
112:   def attach_quote!(status)
113:     return if @quoted_status.nil?
114: 
115:     status.quote = Quote.create(quoted_status: @quoted_status, status: status)
116:     status.quote.ensure_quoted_access
117: 
118:     status.quote.accept! if @quoted_status.local? && StatusPolicy.new(@status.account, @quoted_status).quote?
119:   end
120: 
121:   def attach_tagged_objects!(status)
122:     ProcessLinksService.new.call(status)
123:   end
124: 
125:   def safeguard_mentions!(status)
126:     return if @options[:allowed_mentions].nil?
127: 
128:     expected_account_ids = @options[:allowed_mentions].map(&:to_i)
129: 
130:     unexpected_accounts = status.mentions.map(&:account).to_a.reject { |mentioned_account| expected_account_ids.include?(mentioned_account.id) }
131:     return if unexpected_accounts.empty?
132: 
133:     raise UnexpectedMentionsError.new('Post would be sent to unexpected accounts', unexpected_accounts)
134:   end
135: 
136:   def schedule_status!
137:     status_for_validation = @account.statuses.build(status_attributes)
138:     safeguard_private_mention_quote!(status_for_validation)
139: 
140:     antispam = Antispam.new(status_for_validation)
141:     antispam.local_preflight_check!
142: 
143:     if status_for_validation.valid?
144:       # Marking the status as destroyed is necessary to prevent the status from being
145:       # persisted when the associated media attachments get updated when creating the
146:       # scheduled status.
147:       status_for_validation.destroy
148: 
149:       # The following transaction block is needed to wrap the UPDATEs to
150:       # the media attachments when the scheduled status is created
151: 
152:       ApplicationRecord.transaction do
153:         @status = @account.scheduled_statuses.create!(scheduled_status_attributes)
154:       end
155:     else
156:       raise ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid
157:     end
158:   rescue Antispam::SilentlyDrop
159:     @status = @account.scheduled_status.new(scheduled_status_attributes).tap(&:delete)
160:   end
161: 
162:   def postprocess_status!
163:     process_hashtags_service.call(@status)
164:     Trends.tags.register(@status)
165:     LinkCrawlWorker.perform_async(@status.id)
166:     DistributionWorker.perform_async(@status.id)
167:     process_email_subscriptions!
168:     ActivityPub::DistributionWorker.perform_async(@status.id)
169:     PollExpirationNotifyWorker.perform_at(@status.poll.expires_at, @status.poll.id) if @status.poll
170:     ActivityPub::QuoteRequestWorker.perform_async(@status.quote.id) if @status.quote&.quoted_status.present? && !@status.quote&.quoted_status&.local?
171:   end
172: 
173:   def process_email_subscriptions!
174:     return unless Rails.application.config.x.email_subscriptions && Setting.email_subscriptions &&
175:                   @status.public_visibility? && (!@status.reply? || @status.in_reply_to_account_id == @status.account_id) &&
176:                   @status.account.user_can?(:manage_email_subscriptions) &&
177:                   @status.account.user_email_subscriptions_enabled?
178: 
179:     # To allow e-mail grouping, pass the arguments via a redis set and schedule
180:     # a unique worker a few minutes in the future, in case the user makes subsequent
181:     # posts within that time window
182:     redis.sadd("email_subscriptions:#{@status.account_id}:next_batch", @status.id)
183:     redis.expire("email_subscriptions:#{@status.account_id}:next_batch", EMAIL_DISTRIBUTION_TTL)
184:     EmailDistributionWorker.perform_in(EMAIL_DISTRIBUTION_DELAY, @status.account_id)
185:   end
186: 
187:   def validate_media!
188:     if @options[:media_ids].blank? || !@options[:media_ids].is_a?(Enumerable)
189:       @media = []
190:       return
191:     end
192: 
193:     raise Mastodon::ValidationError, I18n.t('media_attachments.validations.too_many') if @options[:media_ids].size > Status::MEDIA_ATTACHMENTS_LIMIT
194: 
195:     @media = @account.media_attachments.where(status_id: nil).where(id: @options[:media_ids].take(Status::MEDIA_ATTACHMENTS_LIMIT).map(&:to_i))
196: 
197:     not_found_ids = @options[:media_ids].map(&:to_i) - @media.map(&:id)
198:     raise Mastodon::ValidationError, I18n.t('media_attachments.validations.not_found', ids: not_found_ids.join(', ')) if not_found_ids.any?
199: 
200:     raise Mastodon::ValidationError, I18n.t('media_attachments.validations.images_and_video') if @media.size > 1 && @media.find(&:audio_or_video?)
201:     raise Mastodon::ValidationError, I18n.t('media_attachments.validations.not_ready') if @media.any?(&:not_processed?)
202:   end
203: 
204:   def process_mentions_service
205:     ProcessMentionsService.new
206:   end
207: 
208:   def process_hashtags_service
209:     ProcessHashtagsService.new
210:   end
211: 
212:   def scheduled?
213:     @scheduled_at.present?
214:   end
215: 
216:   def idempotency_key
217:     "idempotency:status:#{@account.id}:#{@options[:idempotency]}"
218:   end
219: 
220:   def idempotency_given?
221:     @options[:idempotency].present?
222:   end
223: 
224:   def idempotency_duplicate
225:     if scheduled?
226:       @account.scheduled_statuses.find(@idempotency_duplicate)
227:     else
228:       @account.statuses.find(@idempotency_duplicate)
229:     end
230:   end
231: 
232:   def idempotency_duplicate?
233:     @idempotency_duplicate = redis.get(idempotency_key)
234:   end
235: 
236:   def with_idempotency
237:     return yield unless idempotency_given?
238: 
239:     with_redis_lock("idempotency:lock:status:#{@account.id}:#{@options[:idempotency]}") do
240:       return idempotency_duplicate if idempotency_duplicate?
241: 
242:       yield
243: 
244:       redis.setex(idempotency_key, 3_600, @status.id)
245:     end
246:   end
247: 
248:   def scheduled_in_the_past?
249:     @scheduled_at.present? && @scheduled_at <= Time.now.utc
250:   end
251: 
252:   def bump_potential_friendship!
253:     return if !@status.reply? || @account.id == @status.in_reply_to_account_id
254: 
255:     ActivityTracker.increment('activity:interactions')
256:   end
257: 
258:   def status_attributes
259:     {
260:       text: @text,
261:       media_attachments: @media || [],
262:       ordered_media_attachment_ids: (@options[:media_ids] || []).map(&:to_i) & @media.map(&:id),
263:       thread: @in_reply_to,
264:       poll_attributes: poll_attributes,
265:       sensitive: @sensitive,
266:       spoiler_text: @options[:spoiler_text] || '',
267:       visibility: @visibility,
268:       language: valid_locale_cascade(@options[:language], @account.user&.preferred_posting_language, I18n.default_locale),
269:       application: @options[:application],
270:       rate_limit: @options[:with_rate_limit],
271:       quote_approval_policy: @options[:quote_approval_policy],
272:     }.compact
273:   end
274: 
275:   def scheduled_status_attributes
276:     {
277:       scheduled_at: @scheduled_at,
278:       media_attachments: @media || [],
279:       params: scheduled_options,
280:     }
281:   end
282: 
283:   def poll_attributes
284:     return if @options[:poll].blank?
285: 
286:     @options[:poll].merge(account: @account, voters_count: 0)
287:   end
288: 
289:   def scheduled_options
290:     @options.dup.tap do |options_hash|
291:       options_hash[:in_reply_to_id]  = options_hash.delete(:thread)&.id
292:       options_hash[:application_id]  = options_hash.delete(:application)&.id
293:       options_hash[:quoted_status_id] = options_hash.delete(:quoted_status)&.id
294:       options_hash[:scheduled_at]    = nil
295:       options_hash[:idempotency]     = nil
296:       options_hash[:with_rate_limit] = false
297:     end
298:   end
299: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'singleton'
4: 
5: class FeedManager
6:   include Singleton
7:   include Redisable
8: 
9:   # Maximum number of items stored in a single feed
10:   MAX_ITEMS = 800
11: 
12:   # Number of items in the feed since last reblog of status
13:   # before the new reblog will be inserted. Must be <= MAX_ITEMS
14:   # or the tracking sets will grow forever
15:   REBLOG_FALLOFF = 40
16: 
17:   # Execute block for every active account
18:   # @yield [Account]
19:   # @return [void]
20:   def with_active_accounts(&block)
21:     Account.joins(:user).merge(User.signed_in_recently).find_each(&block)
22:   end
23: 
24:   # Redis key of a feed
25:   # @param [Symbol] type
26:   # @param [Integer] id
27:   # @param [Symbol] subtype
28:   # @return [String]
29:   def key(type, id, subtype = nil)
30:     return "feed:#{type}:#{id}" unless subtype
31: 
32:     "feed:#{type}:#{id}:#{subtype}"
33:   end
34: 
35:   # The number of items in the given timeline
36:   # @param [Symbol] type
37:   # @param [Integer] id
38:   # @param [Symbol] subtype
39:   # @return [Integer]
40:   def timeline_size(type, id, subtype = nil)
41:     redis.zcard(key(type, id, subtype))
42:   end
43: 
44:   # The filter result of the status to a particular feed
45:   # @param [Symbol] timeline_type
46:   # @param [Status] status
47:   # @param [Account|List] receiver
48:   # @return [void|Symbol] nil, :filter, or :skip_home
49:   def filter(timeline_type, status, receiver)
50:     case timeline_type
51:     when :home
52:       filter_from_home(status, receiver.id, build_crutches(receiver.id, [status]), :home)
53:     when :list
54:       (filter_from_list?(status, receiver) ? :filter : nil) || filter_from_home(status, receiver.account_id, build_crutches(receiver.account_id, [status], list: receiver), :list)
55:     when :mentions
56:       filter_from_mentions?(status, receiver.id) ? :filter : nil
57:     when :tags
58:       filter_from_tags?(status, receiver.id, build_crutches(receiver.id, [status])) ? :filter : nil
59:     end
60:   end
61: 
62:   # Check if the status should not be added to a feed
63:   # @param [Symbol] timeline_type
64:   # @param [Status] status
65:   # @param [Account|List] receiver
66:   # @return [Boolean]
67:   def filter?(timeline_type, status, receiver)
68:     !!filter(timeline_type, status, receiver)
69:   end
70: 
71:   # Add a status to a home feed and send a streaming API update
72:   # @param [Account] account
73:   # @param [Status] status
74:   # @param [Boolean] update
75:   # @return [Boolean]
76:   def push_to_home(account, status, update: false)
77:     return false unless account.user&.signed_in_recently?

368:     reblogged_id_sets = {}

370:     redis.pipelined do |pipeline|
371:       ids.each do |feed_id|
372:         reblog_key = key(type, feed_id, 'reblogs')
373:         # We collect a future for this: we don't block while getting
374:         # it, but we can iterate over it later.
375:         reblogged_id_sets[feed_id] = pipeline.zrange(reblog_key, 0, -1)
376:         pipeline.del(key(type, feed_id), reblog_key)
377:       end

381:     # references to.
382:     keys_to_delete = reblogged_id_sets.flat_map do |feed_id, future|
383:       future.value.map do |reblogged_id|
384:         key(type, feed_id, "reblogs:#{reblogged_id}")
385:       end
386:     end
387: 
388:     redis.del(keys_to_delete) unless keys_to_delete.empty?

528:   # Adds a status to an account's feed, returning true if a status was
529:   # added, and false if it was not added to the feed. Note that this is
530:   # an internal helper: callers must call trim or push updates if
531:   # either action is appropriate.
532:   # @param [Symbol] timeline_type
533:   # @param [Integer] account_id
534:   # @param [Status] status
535:   # @param [Boolean] aggregate_reblogs
536:   # @return [Boolean]
537:   def add_to_feed(timeline_type, account_id, status, aggregate_reblogs: true)
538:     timeline_key = key(timeline_type, account_id)
539:     reblog_key   = key(timeline_type, account_id, 'reblogs')
540: 
541:     if status.reblog? && (aggregate_reblogs.nil? || aggregate_reblogs)
542:       # If the original status or a reblog of it is within
543:       # REBLOG_FALLOFF statuses from the top, do not re-insert it into
544:       # the feed
545:       rank = redis.zrevrank(timeline_key, status.reblog_of_id)
546: 
547:       return false if !rank.nil? && rank < FeedManager::REBLOG_FALLOFF
548: 
549:       # The ordered set at `reblog_key` holds statuses which have a reblog
550:       # in the top `REBLOG_FALLOFF` statuses of the timeline
551:       if redis.zadd(reblog_key, status.id, status.reblog_of_id, nx: true)
552:         # This is not something we've already seen reblogged, so we
553:         # can just add it to the feed (and note that we're reblogging it).
554:         redis.zadd(timeline_key, status.id, status.id)
555:       else
556:         # Another reblog of the same status was already in the
557:         # REBLOG_FALLOFF most recent statuses, so we note that this
558:         # is an "extra" reblog, by storing it in reblog_set_key.
559:         reblog_set_key = key(timeline_type, account_id, "reblogs:#{status.reblog_of_id}")
560:         redis.sadd(reblog_set_key, status.id)
561:         return false
562:       end
563:     else
564:       # A reblog may reach earlier than the original status because of the
565:       # delay of the worker delivering the original status, the late addition
566:       # by merging timelines, and other reasons.
567:       # If such a reblog already exists, just do not re-insert it into the feed.
568:       return false unless redis.zscore(reblog_key, status.id).nil?
569: 
570:       redis.zadd(timeline_key, status.id, status.id)
571:     end
572: 
573:     true
574:   end

3: class NotifyService < BaseService
4:   include Redisable
5: 
6:   # TODO: the severed_relationships and annual_report types probably warrants email notifications
7:   NON_EMAIL_TYPES = %i(
8:     admin.report
9:     admin.sign_up
10:     update
11:     quoted_update
12:     poll
13:     status
14:     moderation_warning
15:     severed_relationships
16:     annual_report
17:     added_to_collection
18:     collection_update
19:   ).freeze
20: 
21:   class BaseCondition
22:     NEW_ACCOUNT_THRESHOLD = 30.days.freeze
23: 
24:     NEW_FOLLOWER_THRESHOLD = 3.days.freeze
25: 
26:     def initialize(notification, **options)
27:       @recipient = notification.account
28:       @sender = notification.from_account
29:       @notification = notification
30:       @policy = NotificationPolicy.find_or_initialize_by(account: @recipient)
31:       @from_staff = @sender.local? && @sender.user.present? && @sender.user_role&.bypass_block?(@recipient.user_role)
32:       @options = options
33:     end
34: 
35:     private
36: 
37:     def filterable_type?
38:       Notification::PROPERTIES[@notification.type][:filterable]
39:     end
40: 
41:     def not_following?
42:       !@recipient.following?(@sender)
43:     end
44: 
45:     def not_follower?
46:       follow = Follow.find_by(account: @sender, target_account: @recipient)
47:       follow.nil? || follow.created_at > NEW_FOLLOWER_THRESHOLD.ago
48:     end
49: 
50:     def new_account?
51:       @sender.created_at > NEW_ACCOUNT_THRESHOLD.ago
52:     end
53: 
54:     def override_for_sender?
55:       NotificationPermission.exists?(account: @recipient, from_account: @sender)
56:     end
57: 
58:     def message?
59:       @notification.type == :mention
60:     end
61: 
62:     def from_staff?
63:       @from_staff
64:     end
65: 
66:     def private_mention_not_in_response?
67:       @notification.type == :mention && @notification.target_status.direct_visibility? && !response_to_recipient?
68:     end
69: 
70:     def response_to_recipient?
71:       return false if @notification.target_status.in_reply_to_id.nil?
72: 
73:       statuses_that_mention_sender.positive?
74:     end
75: 
76:     def statuses_that_mention_sender
77:       # This queries private mentions from the recipient to the sender up in the thread.
78:       # This allows up to 100 messages that do not match in the thread, allowing conversations
79:       # involving multiple people.
80:       Status.count_by_sql([<<~SQL.squish, id: @notification.target_status.in_reply_to_id, recipient_id: @recipient.id, sender_id: @sender.id, depth_limit: 100])
81:         WITH RECURSIVE ancestors(id, in_reply_to_id, mention_id, path, depth) AS (
82:             SELECT s.id, s.in_reply_to_id, m.id, ARRAY[s.id], 0
83:             FROM statuses s
84:             LEFT JOIN mentions m ON m.silent = FALSE AND m.account_id = :sender_id AND m.status_id = s.id
85:             WHERE s.id = :id
86:           UNION ALL
87:             SELECT s.id, s.in_reply_to_id, m.id, ancestors.path || s.id, ancestors.depth + 1
88:             FROM ancestors
89:             JOIN statuses s ON s.id = ancestors.in_reply_to_id
90:             /* early exit if we already have a mention matching our requirements */
91:             LEFT JOIN mentions m ON m.silent = FALSE AND m.account_id = :sender_id AND m.status_id = s.id AND s.account_id = :recipient_id
92:             WHERE ancestors.mention_id IS NULL AND NOT s.id = ANY(path) AND ancestors.depth < :depth_limit
93:         )
94:         SELECT COUNT(*)
95:         FROM ancestors
96:         JOIN statuses s ON s.id = ancestors.id
97:         WHERE ancestors.mention_id IS NOT NULL AND s.account_id = :recipient_id AND s.visibility = 3
98:       SQL
99:     end
100: 
101:     def from_bot?
102:       @sender.bot?
103:     end
104:   end

106:   class DropCondition < BaseCondition
107:     def drop?
108:       blocked   = @recipient.unavailable?
109:       blocked ||= from_self? && %i(poll severed_relationships moderation_warning annual_report).exclude?(@notification.type)
110: 
111:       return blocked if message? && from_staff?
112: 
113:       blocked ||= domain_blocking?
114:       blocked ||= @recipient.blocking?(@sender)
115:       blocked ||= @recipient.muting_notifications?(@sender)
116:       blocked ||= conversation_muted?
117:       blocked ||= blocked_mention? if message?
118: 
119:       return true if blocked
120:       return false unless filterable_type?
121:       return false if override_for_sender?
122: 
123:       blocked_by_limited_accounts_policy? ||
124:         blocked_by_not_following_policy? ||
125:         blocked_by_not_followers_policy? ||
126:         blocked_by_new_accounts_policy? ||
127:         blocked_by_private_mentions_policy? ||
128:         blocked_by_bots_policy?
129:     end

131:     private
132: 
133:     def blocked_mention?
134:       FeedManager.instance.filter?(:mentions, @notification.target_status, @recipient)
135:     end
136: 
137:     def from_self?
138:       @recipient.id == @sender.id
139:     end
140: 
141:     def domain_blocking?
142:       @recipient.domain_blocking?(@sender.domain) && not_following?
143:     end
144: 
145:     def conversation_muted?
146:       @notification.target_status && @recipient.muting_conversation?(@notification.target_status.conversation)
147:     end
148: 
149:     def blocked_by_not_following_policy?
150:       @policy.drop_not_following? && not_following?
151:     end
152: 
153:     def blocked_by_not_followers_policy?
154:       @policy.drop_not_followers? && not_follower?
155:     end
156: 
157:     def blocked_by_new_accounts_policy?
158:       @policy.drop_new_accounts? && new_account? && not_following?
159:     end
160: 
161:     def blocked_by_private_mentions_policy?
162:       @policy.drop_private_mentions? && not_following? && private_mention_not_in_response?
163:     end
164: 
165:     def blocked_by_limited_accounts_policy?
166:       @policy.drop_limited_accounts? && (@options[:silenced] || @sender.silenced?) && not_following?
167:     end
168: 
169:     def blocked_by_bots_policy?
170:       @policy.drop_bots? && from_bot? && not_following?
171:     end
172:   end

174:   class FilterCondition < BaseCondition
175:     def filter?
176:       return false unless filterable_type?
177:       return false if override_for_sender?
178:       return false if message? && from_staff?
179: 
180:       filtered_by_limited_accounts_policy? ||
181:         filtered_by_not_following_policy? ||
182:         filtered_by_not_followers_policy? ||
183:         filtered_by_new_accounts_policy? ||
184:         filtered_by_private_mentions_policy? ||
185:         filtered_by_bots_policy?
186:     end
187: 
188:     private
189: 
190:     def filtered_by_not_following_policy?
191:       @policy.filter_not_following? && not_following?
192:     end
193: 
194:     def filtered_by_not_followers_policy?
195:       @policy.filter_not_followers? && not_follower?
196:     end
197: 
198:     def filtered_by_new_accounts_policy?
199:       @policy.filter_new_accounts? && new_account? && not_following?
200:     end
201: 
202:     def filtered_by_private_mentions_policy?
203:       @policy.filter_private_mentions? && not_following? && private_mention_not_in_response?
204:     end
205: 
206:     def filtered_by_limited_accounts_policy?
207:       @policy.filter_limited_accounts? && (@options[:silenced] || @sender.silenced?) && not_following?
208:     end
209: 
210:     def filtered_by_bots_policy?
211:       @policy.filter_bots? && from_bot? && not_following?
212:     end
213:   end

215:   def call(recipient, type, activity, **options)
216:     return if recipient.user.nil?
217: 
218:     @options      = options
219:     @recipient    = recipient
220:     @activity     = activity
221:     @notification = Notification.new(account: @recipient, type: type, activity: @activity)
222: 
223:     # For certain conditions we don't need to create a notification at all
224:     return if drop?
225: 
226:     @notification.filtered = filter?
227:     @notification.set_group_key!
228:     @notification.save!
229: 
230:     # It's possible the underlying activity has been deleted
231:     # between the save call and now
232:     return if @notification.activity.nil?
233: 
234:     if @notification.filtered?
235:       update_notification_request!
236:     else
237:       push_notification!
238:       push_to_conversation! if direct_message?
239:       send_email! if email_needed?
240:     end
241:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid
242:     nil
243:   end
244: 
245:   private
246: 
247:   def drop?
248:     DropCondition.new(@notification, silenced: @options[:silenced]).drop?
249:   end
250: 
251:   def filter?
252:     FilterCondition.new(@notification, silenced: @options[:silenced]).filter?
253:   end
254: 
255:   def update_notification_request!
256:     return unless %i(mention quote).include?(@notification.type)
257: 
258:     notification_request = NotificationRequest.find_or_initialize_by(account_id: @recipient.id, from_account_id: @notification.from_account_id)
259:     notification_request.last_status_id = @notification.target_status.id
260:     notification_request.save
261:   end
262: 
263:   def push_notification!
264:     push_to_streaming_api! if subscribed_to_streaming_api?
265:     push_to_web_push_subscriptions!
266:   end
267: 
268:   def push_to_streaming_api!
269:     redis.publish("timeline:#{@recipient.id}:notifications", { event: :notification, payload: InlineRenderer.render(@notification, @recipient, :notification) }.to_json)
270:   end
271: 
272:   def subscribed_to_streaming_api?
273:     redis.exists?("subscribed:timeline:#{@recipient.id}") || redis.exists?("subscribed:timeline:#{@recipient.id}:notifications")
274:   end
275: 
276:   def push_to_conversation!
277:     AccountConversation.add_status(@recipient, @notification.target_status)
278:   end
279: 
280:   def direct_message?
281:     @notification.type == :mention && @notification.target_status.direct_visibility?
282:   end
283: 
284:   def push_to_web_push_subscriptions!
285:     ::Web::PushNotificationWorker.push_bulk(web_push_subscriptions.select { |subscription| subscription.pushable?(@notification) }) { |subscription| [subscription.id, @notification.id] }

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'zip'
4: 
5: class BackupService < BaseService
6:   include Payloadable
7:   include ContextHelper
8: 
9:   CHUNK_SIZE = 1.megabyte
10:   PLACEHOLDER = '!PLACEHOLDER!'
11: 
12:   STREAM_ACTOR = 'actor.json'
13:   STREAM_BOOKMARKS = 'bookmarks.json'
14:   STREAM_LIKES = 'likes.json'
15:   STREAM_OUTBOX = 'outbox.json'
16: 
17:   attr_reader :account, :backup
18: 
19:   def call(backup)
20:     @backup  = backup
21:     @account = backup.user.account
22: 
23:     build_archive!
24:   end
25: 
26:   private
27: 
28:   def build_outbox_json!(file)
29:     skeleton = serialize(collection_presenter(STREAM_OUTBOX, size: account.statuses.count), ActivityPub::CollectionSerializer)
30:     skeleton[:@context] = full_context
31:     skeleton[:orderedItems] = [PLACEHOLDER]
32:     skeleton = skeleton.to_json
33:     prepend, append = skeleton.split(PLACEHOLDER.to_json)
34: 
35:     file.write(prepend)
36: 
37:     account.statuses.with_includes.reorder(nil).find_in_batches.with_index do |statuses, batch|
38:       file.write(',') unless batch.zero?
39: 
40:       file.write(statuses.map do |status|
41:         serializer = status.reblog? ? ActivityPub::AnnounceNoteSerializer : ActivityPub::CreateNoteSerializer
42:         item = serialize_payload(status, serializer)
43:         item.delete(:@context)
44: 
45:         unless item[:type] == 'Announce' || item[:object][:attachment].blank?
46:           item[:object][:attachment].each do |attachment|
47:             attachment[:url] = Addressable::URI.parse(attachment[:url]).path.delete_prefix('/system/')
48:           end
49:         end
50: 
51:         item.to_json
52:       end.join(','))
53: 
54:       GC.start
55:     end
56: 
57:     file.write(append)
58:   end
59: 
60:   def build_archive!
61:     tmp_file = Tempfile.new(%w(archive .zip))
62: 
63:     build_zip_file(tmp_file)
64: 
65:     @backup.dump = ActionDispatch::Http::UploadedFile.new(tempfile: tmp_file, filename: archive_filename)
66:     @backup.processed = true
67:     @backup.save!
68:   ensure
69:     tmp_file.close
70:     tmp_file.unlink
71:   end
72: 
73:   def build_zip_file(file)
74:     Zip::File.open(file, create: true) do |zip|
75:       dump_outbox!(zip)
76:       dump_media_attachments!(zip)
77:       dump_likes!(zip)
78:       dump_bookmarks!(zip)
79:       dump_actor!(zip)
80:     end
81:   end
82: 
83:   def archive_filename
84:     "#{archive_id}.zip"
85:   end
86: 
87:   def archive_id
88:     [:archive, Time.current.to_fs(:number), SecureRandom.hex(16)].join('-')
89:   end
90: 
91:   def dump_media_attachments!(zipfile)
92:     MediaAttachment.attached.where(account: account).find_in_batches do |media_attachments|
93:       media_attachments.each do |m|
94:         path = m.file&.path
95:         next unless path
96: 
97:         path = path.gsub(%r{\A.*/system/}, '')
98:         path = path.gsub(%r{\A/+}, '')
99:         download_to_zip(zipfile, m.file, path)
100:       end
101: 
102:       GC.start
103:     end
104:   end
105: 
106:   def dump_outbox!(zipfile)
107:     zipfile.get_output_stream(STREAM_OUTBOX) do |io|
108:       build_outbox_json!(io)
109:     end
110:   end
111: 
112:   def dump_actor!(zipfile)
113:     actor = serialize(account, ActivityPub::ActorSerializer)
114: 
115:     actor[:icon][:url]  = "avatar#{File.extname(actor[:icon][:url])}"  if actor[:icon]
116:     actor[:image][:url] = "header#{File.extname(actor[:image][:url])}" if actor[:image]
117:     actor[:outbox]      = STREAM_OUTBOX
118:     actor[:likes]       = STREAM_LIKES
119:     actor[:bookmarks]   = STREAM_BOOKMARKS
120: 
121:     download_to_zip(zipfile, account.avatar, "avatar#{File.extname(account.avatar.path)}") if account.avatar.exists?
122:     download_to_zip(zipfile, account.header, "header#{File.extname(account.header.path)}") if account.header.exists?
123: 
124:     zipfile.get_output_stream(STREAM_ACTOR) do |io|
125:       io.write(actor.to_json)
126:     end
127:   end
128: 
129:   def dump_likes!(zipfile)
130:     skeleton = serialize(collection_presenter(STREAM_LIKES), ActivityPub::CollectionSerializer)
131: 
132:     skeleton.delete(:totalItems)
133:     skeleton[:orderedItems] = [PLACEHOLDER]
134:     skeleton = skeleton.to_json
135:     prepend, append = skeleton.split(PLACEHOLDER.to_json)
136: 
137:     zipfile.get_output_stream(STREAM_LIKES) do |io|
138:       io.write(prepend)
139: 
140:       favourite_statuses.find_in_batches.with_index do |statuses, batch|
141:         io.write(',') unless batch.zero?
142: 
143:         io.write(statuses.map do |status|
144:           ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(status).to_json
145:         end.join(','))
146: 
147:         GC.start
148:       end
149: 
150:       io.write(append)
151:     end
152:   end
153: 
154:   def favourite_statuses
155:     Status.reorder(nil).joins(:favourites).includes(:account).merge(account.favourites)
156:   end
157: 
158:   def dump_bookmarks!(zipfile)
159:     skeleton = serialize(collection_presenter(STREAM_BOOKMARKS), ActivityPub::CollectionSerializer)
160:     skeleton.delete(:totalItems)
161:     skeleton[:orderedItems] = [PLACEHOLDER]
162:     skeleton = skeleton.to_json
163:     prepend, append = skeleton.split(PLACEHOLDER.to_json)
164: 
165:     zipfile.get_output_stream(STREAM_BOOKMARKS) do |io|
166:       io.write(prepend)
167: 
168:       bookmark_statuses.find_in_batches.with_index do |statuses, batch|
169:         io.write(',') unless batch.zero?
170: 
171:         io.write(statuses.map do |status|
172:           ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(status).to_json
173:         end.join(','))
174: 
175:         GC.start
176:       end
177: 
178:       io.write(append)
179:     end
180:   end
181: 
182:   def bookmark_statuses
183:     Status.reorder(nil).joins(:bookmarks).includes(:account).merge(account.bookmarks)
184:   end
185: 
186:   def collection_presenter(id, size: 0)
187:     ActivityPub::CollectionPresenter.new(
188:       id:,
189:       items: [],
190:       size:,
191:       type: :ordered
192:     )
193:   end
194: 
195:   def serialize(object, serializer)
196:     ActiveModelSerializers::SerializableResource.new(
197:       object,
198:       serializer: serializer,
199:       adapter: ActivityPub::Adapter
200:     ).as_json
201:   end
202: 
203:   def download_to_zip(zipfile, attachment, filename)
204:     adapter = Paperclip.io_adapters.for(attachment)
205: 
206:     zipfile.get_output_stream(filename) do |io|
207:       while (buffer = adapter.read(CHUNK_SIZE))
208:         io.write(buffer)
209:       end
210:     end
211:   rescue Errno::ENOENT, Seahorse::Client::NetworkingError => e
212:     Rails.logger.warn "Could not backup file #{filename}: #{e}"
213:   end
214: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Notification::Groups
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   # `set_group_key!` needs to be updated if this list changes
7:   GROUPABLE_NOTIFICATION_TYPES = %i(favourite reblog follow admin.sign_up).freeze
8:   MAXIMUM_GROUP_SPAN_HOURS = 12
9: 
10:   included do
11:     scope :by_group_key, ->(group_key) { group_key&.start_with?('ungrouped-') ? where(id: group_key.delete_prefix('ungrouped-')) : where(group_key: group_key) }
12:   end
13: 
14:   def set_group_key!
15:     return if filtered? || GROUPABLE_NOTIFICATION_TYPES.exclude?(type)
16: 
17:     type_prefix = case type
18:                   when :favourite, :reblog
19:                     [type, target_status&.id].join('-')
20:                   when :follow, :'admin.sign_up'
21:                     type
22:                   else
23:                     raise NotImplementedError
24:                   end
25:     redis_key   = "notif-group/#{account.id}/#{type_prefix}"
26:     hour_bucket = activity.created_at.utc.to_i / 1.hour.to_i
27: 
28:     # Reuse previous group if it does not span too large an amount of time
29:     previous_bucket = redis.get(redis_key).to_i
30:     hour_bucket = previous_bucket if hour_bucket < previous_bucket + MAXIMUM_GROUP_SPAN_HOURS
31: 
32:     # We do not concern ourselves with race conditions since we use hour buckets
33:     redis.set(redis_key, hour_bucket, ex: MAXIMUM_GROUP_SPAN_HOURS.hours.to_i)
34: 
35:     self.group_key = "#{type_prefix}-#{hour_bucket}"
36:   end
37: 
38:   class_methods do
39:     def paginate_groups(limit, pagination_order, grouped_types: nil)
40:       raise ArgumentError unless %i(asc desc).include?(pagination_order)
41: 
42:       query = reorder(id: pagination_order)
43: 
44:       # Ideally `:types` would be a bind rather than part of the SQL itself, but that does not
45:       # seem to be possible to do with Rails, considering that the expression would occur in
46:       # multiple places, including in a `select`
47:       group_key_sql = begin
48:         if grouped_types.present?
49:           # Normalize `grouped_types` so the number of different SQL query shapes remains small, and
50:           # the queries can be analyzed in monitoring/telemetry tools
51:           grouped_types = (grouped_types.map(&:to_sym) & GROUPABLE_NOTIFICATION_TYPES).sort
52: 
53:           sanitize_sql_array([<<~SQL.squish, { types: grouped_types }])
54:             COALESCE(
55:               CASE
56:                 WHEN notifications.type IN (:types) THEN notifications.group_key
57:                 ELSE NULL
58:               END,
59:               'ungrouped-' || notifications.id
60:             )
61:           SQL
62:         else
63:           "COALESCE(notifications.group_key, 'ungrouped-' || notifications.id)"
64:         end
65:       end
66: 
67:       unscoped
68:         .with_recursive(
69:           grouped_notifications: [
70:             # Base case: fetching one notification and annotating it with visited groups
71:             query
72:               .select('notifications.*', "ARRAY[#{group_key_sql}] AS groups")
73:               .limit(1),
74:             # Recursive case, always yielding at most one annotated notification
75:             unscoped
76:               .from(
77:                 [
78:                   # Expose the working table as `wt`, but quit early if we've reached the limit
79:                   unscoped
80:                     .select('id', 'groups')
81:                     .from('grouped_notifications')
82:                     .where('array_length(grouped_notifications.groups, 1) < :limit', limit: limit)
83:                     .arel.as('wt'),
84:                   # Recursive query, using `LATERAL` so we can refer to `wt`
85:                   query
86:                     .where(pagination_order == :desc ? 'notifications.id < wt.id' : 'notifications.id > wt.id')
87:                     .where.not("#{group_key_sql} = ANY(wt.groups)")
88:                     .limit(1)
89:                     .arel.lateral('notifications'),
90:                 ]
91:               )
92:               .select('notifications.*', "array_append(wt.groups, #{group_key_sql}) AS groups"),
93:           ]
94:         )
95:         .from('grouped_notifications AS notifications')
96:         .order(id: pagination_order)
97:         .limit(limit)
98:     end
99: 
100:     # This returns notifications from the request page, but with at most one notification per group.
101:     # Notifications that have no `group_key` each count as a separate group.
102:     def paginate_groups_by_max_id(limit, max_id: nil, since_id: nil, grouped_types: nil)
103:       query = reorder(id: :desc)
104:       query = query.where(id: ...(max_id.to_i)) if max_id.present?
105:       query = query.where(id: (since_id.to_i + 1)...) if since_id.present?
106:       query.paginate_groups(limit, :desc, grouped_types: grouped_types)
107:     end
108: 
109:     # Differs from :paginate_groups_by_max_id in that it gives the results immediately following min_id,
110:     # whereas since_id gives the items with largest id, but with since_id as a cutoff.
111:     # Results will be in ascending order by id.
112:     def paginate_groups_by_min_id(limit, max_id: nil, min_id: nil, grouped_types: nil)
113:       query = reorder(id: :asc)
114:       query = query.where(id: (min_id.to_i + 1)...) if min_id.present?
115:       query = query.where(id: ...(max_id.to_i)) if max_id.present?
116:       query.paginate_groups(limit, :asc, grouped_types: grouped_types)
117:     end
118: 
119:     def to_a_grouped_paginated_by_id(limit, options = {})
120:       if options[:min_id].present?
121:         paginate_groups_by_min_id(limit, min_id: options[:min_id], max_id: options[:max_id], grouped_types: options[:grouped_types]).reverse
122:       else
123:         paginate_groups_by_max_id(limit, max_id: options[:max_id], since_id: options[:since_id], grouped_types: options[:grouped_types]).to_a
124:       end
125:     end
126:   end
127: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class ActivityPub::Activity::Create < ActivityPub::Activity
4:   DISTRIBUTE_DELAY = 1.minute
5:   PROCESSING_DELAY = (30.seconds)..(10.minutes)
6: 
7:   def perform
8:     @account.schedule_refresh_if_stale!
9: 
10:     dereference_object!
11: 
12:     create_status
13:   end
14: 
15:   private
16: 
17:   def create_status
18:     return reject_payload! if unsupported_object_type? || non_matching_uri_hosts?(@account.uri, object_uri) || tombstone_exists? || !related_to_local_activity?
19: 
20:     with_redis_lock("create:#{object_uri}") do
21:       Status.uncached do
22:         return if delete_arrived_first?(object_uri) || poll_vote?
23: 
24:         @status = find_existing_status
25:       end
26: 
27:       if @status.nil?
28:         process_status
29:       elsif @options[:delivered_to_account_id].present?
30:         postprocess_audience_and_deliver
31:       end
32:     end
33: 
34:     @status
35:   end
36: 
37:   def audience_to
38:     as_array(@object['to'] || @json['to']).map { |x| value_or_id(x) }
39:   end
40: 
41:   def audience_cc
42:     as_array(@object['cc'] || @json['cc']).map { |x| value_or_id(x) }
43:   end
44: 
45:   def process_status
46:     @tags                 = []
47:     @mentions             = []
48:     @tagged_objects       = []
49:     @unresolved_mentions  = []
50:     @unresolved_collections = []
51:     @silenced_account_ids = []
52:     @params               = {}
53:     @quote                = nil
54:     @quote_uri            = nil
55:     @quote_approval_uri   = nil
56: 
57:     process_status_params
58:     process_tags
59:     process_quote
60:     process_audience
61: 
62:     ApplicationRecord.transaction do
63:       @status = Status.create!(@params.merge(quote: @quote))
64:       attach_tags(@status)
65:       attach_tagged_objects(@status)
66:       attach_mentions(@status)
67:       attach_counts(@status)
68:     end
69: 
70:     resolve_thread(@status)
71:     resolve_unresolved_mentions(@status)
72:     resolve_unresolved_collections(@status)
73:     fetch_replies(@status)
74:     fetch_and_verify_quote
75:     distribute
76:     forward_for_reply
77:   end
78: 
79:   def distribute
80:     # Spread out crawling randomly to avoid DDoSing the link
81:     LinkCrawlWorker.perform_in(rand(DISTRIBUTE_DELAY), @status.id)
82: 
83:     # Distribute into home and list feeds and notify mentioned accounts
84:     ::DistributionWorker.perform_async(@status.id, { 'silenced_account_ids' => @silenced_account_ids }) if @options[:override_timestamps] || @status.within_realtime_window?
85:   end
86: 
87:   def find_existing_status
88:     status   = status_from_uri(object_uri)
89:     status ||= Status.find_by(uri: @object['atomUri']) if @object['atomUri'].present?
90:     status if status&.account_id == @account.id
91:   end
92: 
93:   def process_status_params
94:     @status_parser = ActivityPub::Parser::StatusParser.new(
95:       @json,
96:       followers_collection: @account.followers_url,
97:       following_collection: @account.following_url,
98:       actor_uri: ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(@account),
99:       object: @object
100:     )
101: 
102:     attachment_ids = process_attachments.take(Status::MEDIA_ATTACHMENTS_LIMIT).map(&:id)
103: 
104:     @params = {
105:       uri: @status_parser.uri,
106:       url: @status_parser.url || @status_parser.uri,
107:       account: @account,
108:       text: @status_parser.processed_text,
109:       language: @status_parser.language,
110:       spoiler_text: @status_parser.processed_spoiler_text,
111:       created_at: @status_parser.created_at,
112:       edited_at: @status_parser.edited_at && @status_parser.edited_at != @status_parser.created_at ? @status_parser.edited_at : nil,
113:       override_timestamps: @options[:override_timestamps],
114:       reply: @status_parser.reply,
115:       sensitive: @account.sensitized? || @status_parser.sensitive || false,
116:       visibility: @status_parser.visibility,
117:       thread: replied_to_status,
118:       conversation: conversation_from_uri(@object['conversation']),
119:       media_attachment_ids: attachment_ids,
120:       ordered_media_attachment_ids: attachment_ids,
121:       poll: process_poll,
122:       quote_approval_policy: @status_parser.quote_policy,
123:     }
124:   end
125: 
126:   def process_audience
127:     # Unlike with tags, there is no point in resolving accounts we don't already
128:     # know here, because silent mentions would only be used for local access control anyway
129:     accounts_in_audience = (audience_to + audience_cc).uniq.filter_map do |audience|
130:       account_from_uri(audience) unless ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.public_collection?(audience)
131:     end
132: 
133:     # If the payload was delivered to a specific inbox, the inbox owner must have
134:     # access to it, unless they already have access to it anyway
135:     if @options[:delivered_to_account_id]
136:       accounts_in_audience << delivered_to_account
137:       accounts_in_audience.uniq!
138:     end
139: 
140:     accounts_in_audience.each do |account|
141:       # This runs after tags are processed, and those translate into non-silent
142:       # mentions, which take precedence
143:       next if @mentions.any? { |mention| mention.account_id == account.id }
144: 
145:       @mentions << Mention.new(account: account, silent: true)
146: 
147:       # If there is at least one silent mention, then the status can be considered
148:       # as a limited-audience status, and not strictly a direct message, but only
149:       # if we considered a direct message in the first place
150:       @params[:visibility] = :limited if @params[:visibility] == :direct
151:     end
152: 
153:     # Accounts that are tagged but are not in the audience are not
154:     # supposed to be notified explicitly
155:     @silenced_account_ids = @mentions.filter_map { |mention| mention.account_id if mention.account.local? } - accounts_in_audience.map(&:id)
156:   end
157: 
158:   def postprocess_audience_and_deliver
159:     return if @status.mentions.find_by(account_id: @options[:delivered_to_account_id])
160: 
161:     @status.mentions.create(account: delivered_to_account, silent: true)
162:     @status.update(visibility: :limited) if @status.direct_visibility?
163: 
164:     return unless delivered_to_account.following?(@account)
165: 
166:     FeedInsertWorker.perform_async(@status.id, delivered_to_account.id, 'home')
167:   end
168: 
169:   def delivered_to_account
170:     @delivered_to_account ||= Account.find(@options[:delivered_to_account_id])
171:   end
172: 
173:   def attach_tags(status)
174:     @tags.each do |tag|
175:       status.tags << tag
176:       tag.update(last_status_at: status.created_at) if tag.last_status_at.nil? || (tag.last_status_at < status.created_at && tag.last_status_at < 12.hours.ago)
177:     end
178: 
179:     # If we're processing an old status, this may register tags as being used now
180:     # as opposed to when the status was really published, but this is probably
181:     # not a big deal
182:     Trends.tags.register(status)
183: 
184:     # Update featured tags
185:     return if @tags.empty? || !status.distributable?
186: 
187:     @account.featured_tags.where(tag_id: @tags.pluck(:id)).find_each do |featured_tag|
188:       featured_tag.increment(status.created_at)
189:     end
190:   end
191: 
192:   def attach_tagged_objects(status)
193:     @tagged_objects.each do |tagged_object|
194:       tagged_object.status = status
195:       tagged_object.save
196:     end
197:   end
198: 
199:   def attach_mentions(status)
200:     @mentions.each do |mention|
201:       mention.status = status
202:       mention.save
203:     end
204:   end
205: 
206:   def attach_counts(status)
207:     likes = @status_parser.favourites_count
208:     shares = @status_parser.reblogs_count
209:     return if likes.nil? && shares.nil?
210: 
211:     status.status_stat.tap do |status_stat|
212:       status_stat.untrusted_reblogs_count = shares unless shares.nil?
213:       status_stat.untrusted_favourites_count = likes unless likes.nil?
214:       status_stat.save if status_stat.changed?
215:     end
216:   end
217: 
218:   def process_tags
219:     return if @object['tag'].nil?
220: 
221:     as_array(@object['tag']).each do |tag|
222:       if equals_or_includes?(tag['type'], 'Hashtag')
223:         process_hashtag tag
224:       elsif equals_or_includes?(tag['type'], 'Mention')
225:         process_mention tag
226:       elsif equals_or_includes?(tag['type'], 'Emoji')
227:         process_emoji tag
228:       elsif equals_or_includes?(tag['type'], 'FeaturedCollection')
229:         process_tagged_collection tag
230:       end
231:     end
232:   end
233: 
234:   def process_quote
235:     @quote_uri = @status_parser.quote_uri
236:     return unless @status_parser.quote?
237: 
238:     @quote_approval_uri = @status_parser.quote_approval_uri
239:     @quote_approval_uri = nil if unsupported_uri_scheme?(@quote_approval_uri) || TagManager.instance.local_url?(@quote_approval_uri)
240:     @quote = Quote.new(account: @account, approval_uri: nil, legacy: @status_parser.legacy_quote?, state: @status_parser.deleted_quote? ? :deleted : :pending)
241:   end
242: 
243:   def process_hashtag(tag)
244:     return if tag['name'].blank?
245: 
246:     Tag.find_or_create_by_names(tag['name']) do |hashtag|
247:       @tags << hashtag unless @tags.include?(hashtag) || !hashtag.valid?
248:     end
249:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid
250:     nil
251:   end
252: 
253:   def process_mention(tag)
254:     return if tag['href'].blank?
255: 
256:     account = account_from_uri(tag['href'])
257:     account = ActivityPub::FetchRemoteAccountService.new.call(tag['href'], request_id: @options[:request_id]) if account.nil?
258: 
259:     return if account.nil?
260: 
261:     @mentions << Mention.new(account: account, silent: false)
262:   rescue Mastodon::UnexpectedResponseError, *Mastodon::HTTP_CONNECTION_ERRORS
263:     @unresolved_mentions << tag['href']
264:   end
265: 
266:   def process_emoji(tag)
267:     return if skip_download?
268: 
269:     custom_emoji_parser = ActivityPub::Parser::CustomEmojiParser.new(tag)
270: 
271:     return if custom_emoji_parser.shortcode.blank? || custom_emoji_parser.image_remote_url.blank?
272: 
273:     emoji = CustomEmoji.find_by(shortcode: custom_emoji_parser.shortcode, domain: @account.domain)
274: 
275:     return unless emoji.nil? || custom_emoji_parser.image_remote_url != emoji.image_remote_url || (custom_emoji_parser.updated_at && custom_emoji_parser.updated_at >= emoji.updated_at)
276: 
277:     begin
278:       emoji ||= CustomEmoji.new(domain: @account.domain, shortcode: custom_emoji_parser.shortcode, uri: custom_emoji_parser.uri)
279:       emoji.image_remote_url = custom_emoji_parser.image_remote_url
280:       emoji.save
281:     rescue Seahorse::Client::NetworkingError => e
282:       Rails.logger.warn "Error storing emoji: #{e}"
283:     end
284:   end
285: 
286:   def process_tagged_collection(tag)
287:     return if tag['id'].blank?
288: 
289:     # TODO: We probably want to resolve unknown objects and push them to an `@unresolved_tagged_objects` on failure
290:     collection = ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_to_resource(tag['id'], Collection)
291:     collection ||= ActivityPub::FetchRemoteFeaturedCollectionService.new.call(tag['id'], request_id: @options[:request_id])
292: 
293:     @tagged_objects << TaggedObject.new(uri: ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(collection), object: collection, ap_type: 'FeaturedCollection') if collection.present?
294:   rescue Mastodon::UnexpectedResponseError, *Mastodon::HTTP_CONNECTION_ERRORS
295:     @unresolved_collections << tag['id']
296:   end
297: 
298:   def process_attachments
299:     return [] if @object['attachment'].nil?
300: 
301:     media_attachments = []
302: 
303:     as_array(@object['attachment']).each do |attachment|
304:       media_attachment_parser = ActivityPub::Parser::MediaAttachmentParser.new(attachment)
305: 
306:       next if media_attachment_parser.remote_url.blank? || media_attachments.size >= Status::MEDIA_ATTACHMENTS_LIMIT
307: 
308:       begin
309:         media_attachment = MediaAttachment.create(
310:           account: @account,
311:           remote_url: media_attachment_parser.remote_url,
312:           thumbnail_remote_url: media_attachment_parser.thumbnail_remote_url,
313:           description: media_attachment_parser.description,
314:           focus: media_attachment_parser.focus,
315:           blurhash: media_attachment_parser.blurhash
316:         )
317: 
318:         media_attachments << media_attachment
319: 
320:         next if unsupported_media_type?(media_attachment_parser.file_content_type) || skip_download?
321: 
322:         media_attachment.download_file!
323:         media_attachment.download_thumbnail!
324:         media_attachment.save
325:       rescue Mastodon::UnexpectedResponseError, *Mastodon::HTTP_CONNECTION_ERRORS
326:         RedownloadMediaWorker.perform_in(rand(PROCESSING_DELAY), media_attachment.id)
327:       rescue Seahorse::Client::NetworkingError => e
328:         Rails.logger.warn "Error storing media attachment: #{e}"
329:         RedownloadMediaWorker.perform_async(media_attachment.id)
330:       end
331:     end
332: 
333:     media_attachments
334:   rescue Addressable::URI::InvalidURIError => e
335:     Rails.logger.debug { "Invalid URL in attachment: #{e}" }
336:     media_attachments
337:   end
338: 
339:   def process_poll
340:     poll_parser = ActivityPub::Parser::PollParser.new(@object)
341: 
342:     return unless poll_parser.valid?
343: 
344:     @account.polls.new(
345:       multiple: poll_parser.multiple,
346:       expires_at: poll_parser.expires_at,
347:       options: poll_parser.options,
348:       cached_tallies: poll_parser.cached_tallies,
349:       voters_count: poll_parser.voters_count
350:     )
351:   end
352: 
353:   def poll_vote?
354:     return false if replied_to_status.nil? || replied_to_status.preloadable_poll.nil? || !replied_to_status.local? || !replied_to_status.preloadable_poll.options.include?(@object['name'])
355: 
356:     poll_vote! unless replied_to_status.preloadable_poll.expired?
357: 
358:     true
359:   end
360: 
361:   def poll_vote!
362:     poll = replied_to_status.preloadable_poll
363:     already_voted = true
364: 
365:     with_redis_lock("vote:#{replied_to_status.poll_id}:#{@account.id}") do
366:       already_voted = poll.votes.exists?(account: @account)
367:       poll.votes.create!(account: @account, choice: poll.options.index(@object['name']), uri: object_uri)
368:     end
369: 
370:     increment_voters_count! unless already_voted
371:     ActivityPub::DistributePollUpdateWorker.perform_in(3.minutes, replied_to_status.id) unless replied_to_status.preloadable_poll.hide_totals?
372:   end
373: 
374:   def resolve_thread(status)
375:     return unless status.reply? && status.thread.nil? && Request.valid_url?(in_reply_to_uri)
376: 
377:     ThreadResolveWorker.perform_async(status.id, in_reply_to_uri, { 'request_id' => @options[:request_id] })
378:   end
379: 
380:   def resolve_unresolved_mentions(status)
381:     @unresolved_mentions.uniq.each do |uri|
382:       MentionResolveWorker.perform_in(rand(PROCESSING_DELAY), status.id, uri, { 'request_id' => @options[:request_id] })
383:     end
384:   end
385: 
386:   def resolve_unresolved_collections(status)
387:     @unresolved_collections.uniq.each do |uri|
388:       TaggedCollectionResolveWorker.perform_in(rand(PROCESSING_DELAY), status.id, uri, { 'request_id' => @options[:request_id] })
389:     end
390:   end
391: 
392:   def fetch_replies(status)
393:     collection = @object['replies']
394:     return if collection.blank?
395: 
396:     replies = ActivityPub::FetchRepliesService.new.call(status.account.uri, collection, allow_synchronous_requests: false, request_id: @options[:request_id])
397:     return unless replies.nil?
398: 
399:     uri = value_or_id(collection)
400:     ActivityPub::FetchRepliesWorker.perform_async(status.id, uri, { 'request_id' => @options[:request_id] }) unless uri.nil?
401:   rescue => e
402:     Rails.logger.warn "Error fetching replies: #{e}"
403:   end
404: 
405:   def fetch_and_verify_quote
406:     return if @quote.nil?
407: 
408:     embedded_quote = safe_prefetched_embed(@account, @status_parser.quoted_object, @json['context'])
409:     ActivityPub::VerifyQuoteService.new.call(@quote, @quote_approval_uri, fetchable_quoted_uri: @quote_uri, prefetched_quoted_object: embedded_quote, request_id: @options[:request_id], depth: @options[:depth])
410:   rescue Mastodon::RecursionLimitExceededError, Mastodon::UnexpectedResponseError, *Mastodon::HTTP_CONNECTION_ERRORS
411:     ActivityPub::RefetchAndVerifyQuoteWorker.perform_in(rand(PROCESSING_DELAY), @quote.id, @quote_uri, { 'request_id' => @options[:request_id], 'approval_uri' => @quote_approval_uri })
412:   end
413: 
414:   def conversation_from_uri(uri)
415:     return nil if uri.nil?
416:     return Conversation.find_by(id: OStatus::TagManager.instance.unique_tag_to_local_id(uri, 'Conversation')) if OStatus::TagManager.instance.local_id?(uri)
417:     return ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_to_resource(uri, Conversation) if ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.local_uri?(uri)
418: 
419:     begin
420:       Conversation.find_or_create_by!(uri: uri)
421:     rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid, ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
422:       retry
423:     end
424:   end
425: 
426:   def replied_to_status
427:     return @replied_to_status if defined?(@replied_to_status)
428: 
429:     if in_reply_to_uri.blank?
430:       @replied_to_status = nil
431:     else
432:       @replied_to_status   = status_from_uri(in_reply_to_uri)
433:       @replied_to_status ||= status_from_uri(@object['inReplyToAtomUri']) if @object['inReplyToAtomUri'].present?
434:       @replied_to_status
435:     end
436:   end
437: 
438:   def in_reply_to_uri
439:     value_or_id(@object['inReplyTo'])
440:   end
441: 
442:   def unsupported_media_type?(mime_type)
443:     mime_type.present? && !MediaAttachment.supported_mime_types.include?(mime_type)
444:   end
445: 
446:   def skip_download?
447:     return @skip_download if defined?(@skip_download)
448: 
449:     @skip_download ||= DomainBlock.reject_media?(@account.domain)
450:   end
451: 
452:   def reply_to_local?
453:     !replied_to_status.nil? && replied_to_status.account.local?
454:   end
455: 
456:   def related_to_local_activity?
457:     fetch? || followed_by_local_accounts? || requested_through_relay? ||
458:       responds_to_followed_account? || addresses_local_accounts?
459:   end
460: 
461:   def responds_to_followed_account?
462:     !replied_to_status.nil? && (replied_to_status.account.local? || replied_to_status.account.passive_relationships.exists?)
463:   end
464: 
465:   def addresses_local_accounts?
466:     return true if @options[:delivered_to_account_id]
467: 
468:     ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uris_to_local_accounts((audience_to + audience_cc).uniq).exists?
469:   end
470: 
471:   def tombstone_exists?
472:     Tombstone.exists?(uri: object_uri)
473:   end
474: 
475:   def forward_for_reply
476:     return unless @status.distributable? && @json['signature'].present? && reply_to_local?
477: 
478:     ActivityPub::RawDistributionWorker.perform_async(JSON.generate(@json), replied_to_status.account_id, [@account.preferred_inbox_url])
479:   end
480: 
481:   def increment_voters_count!
482:     poll = replied_to_status.preloadable_poll
483: 
484:     unless poll.voters_count.nil?
485:       poll.voters_count = poll.voters_count + 1
486:       poll.save
487:     end
488:   rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError
489:     poll.reload
490:     retry
491:   end
492: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class ActivityPub::Activity::Announce < ActivityPub::Activity
4:   def perform
5:     return reject_payload! if delete_arrived_first?(@json['id']) || !related_to_local_activity?
6:     return reject_payload! if @object.nil?
7: 
8:     with_redis_lock("announce:#{value_or_id(@object)}") do
9:       original_status = status_from_object
10: 
11:       return reject_payload! if original_status.nil? || !announceable?(original_status)
12:       return if requested_through_relay?
13: 
14:       @status = Status.find_by(account: @account, reblog: original_status)
15: 
16:       return @status unless @status.nil?
17: 
18:       @status = Status.create!(
19:         account: @account,
20:         reblog: original_status,
21:         uri: @json['id'],
22:         created_at: @json['published'],
23:         override_timestamps: @options[:override_timestamps],
24:         visibility: visibility_from_audience
25:       )
26: 
27:       Trends.register!(@status)
28: 
29:       distribute
30:     end
31: 
32:     @status
33:   end
34: 
35:   private
36: 
37:   def distribute
38:     # Notify the author of the original status if that status is local
39:     LocalNotificationWorker.perform_async(@status.reblog.account_id, @status.id, 'Status', 'reblog') if reblog_of_local_account?(@status) && !reblog_by_following_group_account?(@status)
40: 
41:     # Distribute into home and list feeds
42:     ::DistributionWorker.perform_async(@status.id) if @options[:override_timestamps] || @status.within_realtime_window?
43:   end
44: 
45:   def reblog_of_local_account?(status)
46:     status.reblog? && status.reblog.account.local?
47:   end
48: 
49:   def reblog_by_following_group_account?(status)
50:     status.reblog? && status.account.group? && status.reblog.account.following?(status.account)
51:   end
52: 
53:   def audience_to
54:     as_array(@json['to']).map { |x| value_or_id(x) }
55:   end
56: 
57:   def audience_cc
58:     as_array(@json['cc']).map { |x| value_or_id(x) }
59:   end
60: 
61:   def visibility_from_audience
62:     if audience_to.any? { |to| ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.public_collection?(to) }
63:       :public
64:     elsif audience_cc.any? { |cc| ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.public_collection?(cc) }
65:       :unlisted
66:     elsif audience_to.include?(@account.followers_url)
67:       :private
68:     else
69:       :direct
70:     end
71:   end
72: 
73:   def announceable?(status)
74:     status.account_id == @account.id || status.distributable?
75:   end

77:   def related_to_local_activity?

3: class ActivityPub::Activity::Delete < ActivityPub::Activity
4:   def perform
5:     return delete_person if @account.uri == object_uri
6:     return delete_feature_authorization! unless feature_authorization_from_object.nil?
7: 
8:     delete_object

13:   def delete_person
14:     with_redis_lock("delete_in_progress:#{@account.id}", autorelease: 2.hours, raise_on_failure: false) do
15:       DeleteAccountService.new.call(@account, reserve_username: false, skip_activitypub: true)
16:     end
17:   end
18: 
19:   def delete_object
20:     return if object_uri.nil?
21: 
22:     with_redis_lock("delete_status_in_progress:#{object_uri}", raise_on_failure: false) do
23:       unless non_matching_uri_hosts?(@account.uri, object_uri)
24:         # This lock ensures a concurrent `ActivityPub::Activity::Create` either
25:         # does not create a status at all, or has finished saving it to the
26:         # database before we try to load it.
27:         # Without the lock, `delete_later!` could be called after `delete_arrived_first?`
28:         # and `Status.find` before `Status.create!`
29:         with_redis_lock("create:#{object_uri}") { delete_later!(object_uri) }
30: 
31:         Tombstone.find_or_create_by(uri: object_uri, account: @account)
32:       end
33: 
34:       case @object['type']
35:       when 'QuoteAuthorization'
36:         revoke_quote
37:       when 'Note', 'Question'
38:         delete_status
39:       else
40:         delete_status || revoke_quote
41:       end
42:     end
43:   end
